title_en: Imam at-Tabari: Life, Education, and Scholarly Stature
title_ar: الإمام الطبري: حياته وتعلمه ومكانته العلمية
title_id: Imam at-Tabari: Kehidupan, Pendidikan, dan Kedudukan Ilmiahnya
content_en:
Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Jarir at-Tabari (224–310 AH / 839–923 CE) stands among the most prolific and consequential scholars in the history of Islamic civilization. Born in Amul, in the province of Tabaristan on the southern shores of the Caspian Sea, he left home as a young man to pursue knowledge across the major centers of Islamic learning — Baghdad, Basra, Kufa, al-Fustat in Egypt, and Syria. He traveled to learn from the leading scholars of his generation in hadith, Quran, grammar, history, and law.

At-Tabari's intellectual formation was extraordinary in its breadth. He studied hadith under some of the most distinguished masters of the third Islamic century and received his formation in Quranic sciences from scholars who traced their transmission back to the Companions and Successors. His command of Arabic grammar and literature was equally deep, and he produced important works in multiple disciplines simultaneously.

In jurisprudence, at-Tabari initially followed the Shafi'i school but later developed his own independent legal methodology and founded what came to be known as the Jariri madhab, named after his patronymic. This school attracted followers for some generations but eventually faded, leaving its traces primarily in his massive legal encyclopedia Ikhtilaf al-Fuqaha. The independence of his legal thought reflects a scholarly confidence rooted in his unparalleled mastery of hadith and the transmitted sciences.

At-Tabari lived and worked primarily in Baghdad, where he taught a circle of students and composed his major works until his death at the age of eighty-six. His productivity was remarkable: biographical sources report that he wrote forty pages per day for forty years, a figure that — however understood — reflects the extraordinary volume of his output. His two greatest surviving works are his monumental Tafsir, Jami al-Bayan fi Tawil al-Quran, and his Tarikh ar-Rusul wal-Muluk, both of which remain irreplaceable primary sources for Islamic scholarship.

At-Tabari's scholarly stature was recognized by his contemporaries. He had students who became major scholars in their own right, and his works were copied and transmitted across the Islamic world within his lifetime. Later scholars across all Islamic disciplines — jurists, hadith critics, historians, and Quran commentators — treat his works as foundational references. The respect accorded to him is reflected in the common scholarly designation 'Ibn Jarir,' which identifies him uniquely among Islamic scholars by his father's name.
content_id:
Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Jarir at-Tabari (224–310 AH / 839–923 M) termasuk di antara ulama paling produktif dan berpengaruh dalam sejarah peradaban Islam. Dilahirkan di Amul, di provinsi Tabaristan yang terletak di pesisir selatan Laut Kaspia, beliau meninggalkan kampung halaman sejak masih muda untuk menuntut ilmu di pusat-pusat keilmuan Islam terkemuka — Baghdad, Basra, Kufah, al-Fustat di Mesir, dan Syam. Beliau melakukan perjalanan jauh untuk belajar dari ulama-ulama terkemuka zamannya dalam bidang hadis, Al-Quran, tata bahasa Arab, sejarah, dan fikih.

Pembentukan intelektual at-Tabari luar biasa dalam keluasannya. Beliau mempelajari hadis dari sejumlah guru paling terkemuka pada abad ketiga Islam, dan memperoleh pembentukan dalam ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran dari para ulama yang sanad keilmuan mereka tersambung hingga ke para sahabat dan tabi'in. Penguasaan beliau atas tata bahasa Arab dan kesusastraan sama dalamnya, dan beliau menghasilkan karya-karya penting dalam berbagai disiplin ilmu secara bersamaan.

Dalam bidang fikih, at-Tabari awalnya mengikuti mazhab Syafi'i, namun kemudian mengembangkan metodologi hukum tersendiri yang mandiri dan mendirikan apa yang kemudian dikenal sebagai mazhab Jariri, dinisbatkan kepada kunyah ayahnya. Mazhab ini menarik pengikut selama beberapa generasi namun akhirnya memudar, meninggalkan jejaknya terutama dalam ensiklopedia fikih monumental beliau, Ikhtilaf al-Fuqaha. Kemandirian pemikiran hukum beliau mencerminkan kepercayaan diri seorang sarjana yang berakar pada penguasaan hadis dan ilmu-ilmu naqliyah yang tiada tandingannya.

At-Tabari hidup dan berkarya terutama di Baghdad, di mana beliau mengajar lingkaran murid-muridnya dan mengarang karya-karya besarnya hingga wafat pada usia delapan puluh enam tahun. Produktivitas beliau sungguh luar biasa: sumber-sumber biografis menyebutkan bahwa beliau menulis empat puluh halaman setiap hari selama empat puluh tahun — angka yang, bagaimanapun dipahami, mencerminkan besarnya volume karyanya. Dua karya terbesarnya yang bertahan hingga kini adalah Tafsir monumental beliau, Jami al-Bayan fi Tawil al-Quran, dan kitab Tarikh ar-Rusul wal-Muluk, keduanya tetap menjadi sumber primer yang tak tergantikan dalam keilmuan Islam.

Kedudukan ilmiah at-Tabari diakui oleh para ulama sezamannya. Beliau memiliki murid-murid yang kelak menjadi ulama besar tersendiri, dan karya-karyanya disalin dan ditransmisikan ke seluruh dunia Islam semasa hidupnya. Ulama-ulama generasi berikutnya dari semua disiplin keilmuan Islam — ahli fikih, kritikus hadis, sejarawan, dan ahli tafsir Al-Quran — menjadikan karya-karyanya sebagai referensi fundamental. Penghormatan yang diberikan kepada beliau tercermin dalam sebutan keilmuan yang lazim digunakan, 'Ibn Jarir', yang secara unik mengidentifikasi beliau di antara para ulama Islam berdasarkan nama ayahnya.
